text: Code reviews are a key practice in software development where team members check each other's code before it is added to the project. The main benefit is catching bugs early, which saves time and money since fixing problems later is much harder. Beyond errors, code reviews also promote knowledge sharing. When developers read and discuss each other's work, they learn new techniques and understand different parts of the system. This helps prevent situations where only one person knows how something works. Overall, regular code reviews build stronger teams and improve the quality of the final product.
